stem: A telecommunications communication network uses 2 parallel relays for redundancy. The message delivered requires at least one relay to function. relay 1 has a reliability between 73% and 82% (uncertain due to limited operational data). relay 2 has a reliability of 86%. What is the overall probability that the message delivered?

ground_truth.answer: P(message delivered) ranges from 0.9622 to 0.9748. Point estimate: 0.9678.

solution_steps: P(system works) = 1 - P(all 2 relays fail simultaneously).
At lower bound: P(all fail) = 0.27 × 0.14 = 0.0378. P(works) = 0.9622.
At upper bound: P(all fail) = 0.18 × 0.14 = 0.0252. P(works) = 0.9748.
common_failure_modes: Multiplying individual reliabilities instead of computing 1 - P(all fail)
Using a single reliability for the uncertain component without propagating uncertainty
Treating the system as a series (all must work) rather than parallel (any one suffices)
